summary: Linked Yoshi's ftYs_SpecialHi.c by switching it from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, cleaning its includes, and adjusting nearby static/header ownership for Yoshi SpecialS data. The PR also changed several sdata2 symbols from global ftYs_Init_* names to local @222/@223/@224/@226 labels. decomp.dev reported linked code +1836 bytes, matched data +116 bytes, linked data +24 bytes, and one new .data match for main/melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialS from 65.12% to 100.00%. There was no human review text in the slice.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialHi.c is built as Object(Matching, ...) instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).
Removed the include of ftYs_SpecialHi.static.h from ftYs_SpecialHi.c and added an include of ftYs_SpecialHi.h alongside existing Yoshi headers.
Updated ftYs_SpecialS.c to include ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialS.static.h and removed an extern declaration for ftYs_Unk3_803CED48[].
Removed the ftYs_SpecialHi.h IWYU export include from the renamed SpecialS static header.
Changed sdata2 entries at 0x804D9A40, 0x804D9A44, 0x804D9A48, and 0x804D9A50 from global ftYs_Init_804D9A40/44/48/50 symbols to local compiler-style labels @222, @223, @224, and @226.
smart_moves: The PR did not just flip the build config to Matching; it also corrected header/static-data placement so the matched object layout and symbol ownership were consistent.
Moving the static header from SpecialHi to SpecialS and including it in ftYs_SpecialS.c made ftYs_Unk3_803CED48 a local static definition rather than an extern reference in SpecialS.
Localizing the sdata2 symbols avoided preserving artificial global ftYs_Init_* names for constants that the matching output treated as local labels.
The include cleanup separated the public SpecialHi interface from file-local/static declarations, which is a reusable pattern when turning a nonmatching fighter file into a linked object.
decomp_lessons: When linking a fighter action file, verify nearby static headers are attached to the translation unit that actually owns the static data; stale static headers can block matching or produce incorrect symbol ownership.

A file becoming Matching may require symbol-map scope corrections, not only source-code changes. Global names in config/GALE01/symbols.txt may need to become local @ labels if that is what the compiler output indicates.

Header cleanup can be part of matching: public headers such as ftYs_SpecialHi.h should be included by the source needing declarations, while .static.h headers should remain file-local implementation details.
decomp.dev's match report can reveal side effects outside the PR title: although the PR title focuses on ftYs_SpecialHi.c, the reported new .data match was for ftYs_SpecialS.
naming_conventions: Use character/action source names such as ftYs_SpecialHi.c and ftYs_SpecialS.c for Yoshi special move files.
Use matching public headers like ftYs_SpecialHi.h for external declarations and separate .static.h headers for file-local/static data.
Compiler-generated local data labels in symbols.txt appear as @222, @223, @224, @226 rather than invented global names when the data is local-scope.
Existing unknown/static Yoshi data names in this slice use the ftYs_Unk3_803CED48-style address-based convention.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promoted the target object in configure.py from NonMatching to Matching after source/header/symbol adjustments.
Matched data by relocating a static data definition into the SpecialS static header and including that header from ftYs_SpecialS.c.
Adjusted sdata2 symbol scope and labels to match compiler-local constants at exact addresses 0x804D9A40 through 0x804D9A50.
Removed an extern declaration once the corresponding static data became available through the translation unit's static header.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
Automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 showed linked code 37.97% (+0.05%, +1836 bytes), matched data 38.70% (+0.01%, +116 bytes), and linked data 33.00% (+24 bytes).
Automated report listed one new match: main/melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialS .data, +41 bytes, from 65.12% to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs where a .static.h file was renamed or moved to the translation unit that owns static data.
Search for Yoshi fighter PRs that convert ftYs_* files from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.
Look for symbols.txt changes that convert ft*_Init_* global sdata2 symbols into local @ labels.
Inspect later ftYs_SpecialS matching work to see whether ftYs_Unk3_803CED48 was eventually given a semantic name.
